coin_profile.name: Sushi
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: Sushi (SUSHI) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #439 by market cap.

SUSHI.name: Sushi

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

chart_zones.mos_note: Price above speculative fair value — no margin of safety.

2026.net_issuance_note: Current snapshot only. Historical supply data requires on-chain indexing not available via free APIs.

meta.data_note: Historical time-series data is not available for this token on the free DeFiLlama API. Charts show current year estimates only. Older or smaller protocols may have limited on-chain data coverage.

forecast_meta.growth_basis_note: Annual growth rates derived from quality score 5.7/10 and survivability 50.0%. Base: 7.0% p.a., Bull: 13.0% p.a., Bear: -21.1% p.a.
forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (7.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (5.7/10). Bull (13.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-21.1%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 50.0% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Fee-based methods (Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple) used DeFiLlama protocol fee data to anchor the valuation. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple produced a value below 10% of current price (fees are low relative to market cap) — shown in the breakdown chart but excluded from the median pool. Network Activity (NVT) treats 24h trading volume as a proxy for on-chain utility — it fires when volume is available and the resulting IV falls within 0.1x–10x of current price. Metcalfe Network Value (MC/Volume ratio) confirmed the NVT signal and contributed to the median pool. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment did not fire: FDV/MC is within the acceptable range for this quality tier, so no dilution penalty was applied. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.

SUSHI.iv_method: Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark

watchlist_signal.reason: Strong protocol but price is running significantly above speculative fair value — wait for a pullback before entering.

bull_points: Current market fear environment may create a near-term mean reversion opportunity in Sushi
Any positive news catalyst — exchange listing, partnership, or protocol upgrade — could trigger a sharp near-term rally from current levels
Crypto market sentiment is cyclical — Sushi's short-term setup may improve as macro uncertainty fades
bear_points: Broader crypto market volatility and macro headwinds present near-term downside risk
Regulatory developments in major markets could create short-term selling pressure
Without a near-term catalyst, Sushi may continue to consolidate or drift lower

bull_points: Long-term adoption thesis for Cash-Flow Protocol assets remains structurally intact
As the crypto ecosystem matures, Sushi's established position should compound in value
Continued development activity and ecosystem growth support a constructive long-term view
bear_points: Annual fees of only $0.6M are low relative to market cap, suggesting the protocol has not yet built a durable fee-generating business
FDV/Holders Revenue of 622x means almost none of the protocol's fees flow to token holders — the token lacks direct cash-flow backing
Long-term execution risk remains a key variable for any crypto investment

metric_assessments.fee_growth: Fee Growth is solid, meeting the expectations you would expect for a protocol at this stage.
metric_assessments.capital_efficiency: Capital Efficiency is middling (94.83 MC/fees ratio). The numbers are acceptable but leave room for improvement.
metric_assessments.holder_value_accrual: Holder Value Accrual is solid (16.9 % of fees to holders), meeting the expectations you would expect for a protocol at this stage.
metric_assessments.network_demand: Network Demand is a clear strength (14.64 volume/MC %), placing this token well above most peers on this dimension.
metric_assessments.dilution_control: Dilution Control is a clear strength (93.65 circulating %), placing this token well above most peers on this dimension.
metric_assessments.protocol_maturity: Protocol Maturity is a concern (439 MC rank). This dimension is below par and worth monitoring closely.

quality_narrative.classification_reason: Sushi is rated Speculative because while it has genuine strengths in network demand, it also has a notable weakness in protocol maturity that introduces meaningful uncertainty. Speculative means real potential but less predictable outcomes than a Safe-rated asset.
quality_narrative.investment_narrative: Sushi is a Cash-Flow Protocol with mixed fundamentals, scoring 5.7/10 overall. Its clearest strength is network demand, while protocol maturity remains the main area to watch. Key risks: low protocol maturity score (1/10). Sushi is appropriate for investors with higher risk tolerance and conviction in its long-term growth thesis.
quality_narrative.quality_conclusion: Sushi scores 5.7/10 (Speculative) as a Cash-Flow Protocol. The overall quality profile reflects the protocol's maturity, fee economics, and token supply dynamics relative to peers.

coin_profile.name: lium
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: lium (SN51) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #487 by market cap.

SN51.name: lium

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

chart_zones.mos_note: Price above speculative fair value — no margin of safety.

2026.net_issuance_note: Current snapshot only. Historical supply data requires on-chain indexing not available via free APIs.

meta.data_note: No protocol fee or TVL data found for this token on DeFiLlama. Charts 1 and 2 are unavailable. Charts 3 and 4 show current-year supply and dilution data from CoinGecko.

forecast_meta.growth_basis_note: Annual growth rates derived from quality score 3.3/10 and survivability 23.3%. Base: 3.0% p.a., Bull: 9.0% p.a., Bear: -48.1% p.a.
forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (3.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (3.3/10). Bull (9.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-48.1%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 23.3% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: this token has no DeFiLlama fee tracking. Valuation relies entirely on network activity and market positioning signals. Network Activity (NVT) treats 24h trading volume as a proxy for on-chain utility — it fires when volume is available and the resulting IV falls within 0.1x–10x of current price. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment penalises the large gap between circulating and fully-diluted supply — future token unlocks are expected to create selling pressure. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.
forecast_meta.price_history_note: Historical price data unavailable for this token. Forecast chart shows projected values only.

SN51.iv_method: Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, Dilution Adjustment
